# solution.py
"""Science_Collaboratory / Team Collaboration Manager.

This module implements the collaboration engine that powers the
``Science_Collaboratory`` system: a thread-safe, in-memory backbone that lets a
team of collaborators (human or AI) organise themselves around projects, tasks,
messages, performance metrics and exportable reports.

The public surface is the :class:`TeamCollaborationManager` facade.  It wires
together a set of focused, lock-sharing stores and exposes a cohesive API for:

* **User management** -- register / fetch / update / delete collaborators.
* **Project management** -- create / update / delete projects with cascading
  clean-up of their tasks and messages.
* **Task management** -- create, assign, re-assign, schedule and transition the
  status of work items (with automatic ``started_at`` / ``completed_at``
  bookkeeping).
* **Messaging** -- threaded messages with file attachments, scoped to either a
  project or a task.
* **Performance dashboard** -- completion rate, average completion time, peer
  ratings and a consolidated per-user dashboard.
* **Reporting** -- project progress, team performance and individual reports,
  exportable to CSV or PDF.

Design notes
------------
* **Thread safety.** Every store shares a single re-entrant lock owned by the
  manager, so cross-entity operations are atomic and concurrent callers never
  corrupt the indexes.
* **Encapsulation.** Every getter returns a deep copy of the stored entity, so
  callers can mutate results freely without touching the authoritative store.
* **Identity.** A single global counter hands out monotonically increasing,
  type-agnostic identifiers, guaranteeing IDs are unique across every entity.

The implementation depends only on the Python standard library.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from enum import Enum
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "ScienceCollaboratory",
    "Science_Collaboratory",
    "TaskStatus",
    "UserRole",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",
    "ConflictError",
    "Attachment",
    "User",
    "Project",
    "Task",
    "Message",
    "Rating",
]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle states a task can occupy."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class UserRole(Enum):
    """Authorisation roles a collaborator can hold."""

    ADMIN = "admin"
    MEMBER = "member"
    VIEWER = "viewer"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats for generated reports."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Categories of report the manager can produce."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by this module."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input data fails a validation rule."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an action."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with the current state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain entities
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class User:
    """A collaborator known to the system."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A unit of work that groups tasks and discussion."""

    id: int
    name: str
    description: str
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    owner_id: int
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A single actionable work item inside a project."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    assignee_id: Optional[int]
    deadline: Optional[datetime]
    status: TaskStatus
    created_at: datetime
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """A binary file attached to a message."""

    id: int
    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime
    content: bytes = b""


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A threaded message scoped to either a project or a task."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int]
    task_id: Optional[int]
    parent_id: Optional[int]
    created_at: datetime
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer rating awarded from one collaborator to another."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str
    task_id: Optional[int]
    created_at: datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Stores (data holders that share a single lock)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Store:
    """Base class for the in-memory stores.

    Each store keeps the lock it shares with its siblings so that callers can
    reason about thread-safety at the store boundary if they wish.
    """

    def __init__(self, lock: "threading.RLock") -> None:
        self._lock = lock


class _UserStore(_Store):
    def __init__(self, lock: "threading.RLock") -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self.by_id: Dict[int, User] = {}
        self.by_username: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self.by_email: Dict[str, int] = {}


class _ProjectStore(_Store):
    def __init__(self, lock: "threading.RLock") -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self.by_id: Dict[int, Project] = {}


class _TaskStore(_Store):
    def __init__(self, lock: "threading.RLock") -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self.by_id: Dict[int, Task] = {}
        self.by_project: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}
        self.by_assignee: Dict[int, set] = {}


class _MessageStore(_Store):
    def __init__(self, lock: "threading.RLock") -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self.by_id: Dict[int, Message] = {}
        self.by_project: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}
        self.by_task: Dict[int, List[int]] = {}


class _PerformanceStore(_Store):
    def __init__(self, lock: "threading.RLock") -> None:
        super().__init__(lock)
        self.ratings_by_ratee: Dict[int, List[Rating]] = {}


class _ReportStore(_Store):
    """Reports are computed on demand; this store only carries the lock."""


# Sentinel used to distinguish "argument omitted" from "argument set to None".
_UNSET = object()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager facade
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Facade coordinating every collaboration feature.

    Parameters
    ----------
    now:
        A zero-argument callable returning the "current" :class:`datetime`.
        Injecting a clock makes the manager fully deterministic for testing.
        Defaults to :func:`datetime.datetime.now`.
    """

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None) -> None:
        self._now: Callable[[], datetime] = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._id_counter = 0

        # Stores all share the single manager-owned lock.
        self.users = _UserStore(self._lock)
        self.projects = _ProjectStore(self._lock)
        self.tasks = _TaskStore(self._lock)
        self.messages = _MessageStore(self._lock)
        self.performance = _PerformanceStore(self._lock)
        self.reports = _ReportStore(self._lock)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Internal helpers
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def _next_id(self) -> int:
        """Return the next globally-unique identifier."""
        with self._lock:
            self._id_counter += 1
            return self._id_counter

    @staticmethod
    def _copy(obj: Any) -> Any:
        """Return a deep copy so callers cannot mutate the stored entity."""
        return copy.deepcopy(obj)

    def _require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self.users.by_id.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def _require_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self.projects.by_id.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def _require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks.by_id.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def _require_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self.messages.by_id.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message

    @staticmethod
    def _validate_email(email: str) -> None:
        if not isinstance(email, str) or not email.strip() or "@" not in email:
            raise ValidationError("invalid email address")

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # User management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def register_user(
        self, username: str, email: str, role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER
    ) -> User:
        """Create and store a new user, returning a copy of it."""
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(username, str) or not username.strip():
                raise ValidationError("username must be a non-empty string")
            self._validate_email(email)
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            username = username.strip()
            email = email.strip()
            if username in self.users.by_username:
                raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if email in self.users.by_email:
                raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            user = User(
                id=self._next_id(),
                username=username,
                email=email,
                role=role,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.users.by_id[user.id] = user
            self.users.by_username[username] = user.id
            self.users.by_email[email] = user.id
            return self._copy(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        """Return a copy of the user with ``user_id``."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._copy(self._require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        """Return every user, sorted by id ascending."""
        with self._lock:
            return [
                self._copy(self.users.by_id[uid])
                for uid in sorted(self.users.by_id)
            ]

    def update_user(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        username: Any = _UNSET,
        email: Any = _UNSET,
        role: Any = _UNSET,
    ) -> User:
        """Update mutable fields of a user, returning the updated copy."""
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            if username is not _UNSET:
                if not isinstance(username, str) or not username.strip():
                    raise ValidationError("username must be a non-empty string")
                new_username = username.strip()
                existing = self.users.by_username.get(new_username)
                if existing is not None and existing != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
                if new_username != user.username:
                    self.users.by_username.pop(user.username, None)
                    self.users.by_username[new_username] = user_id
                    user.username = new_username

            if email is not _UNSET:
                self._validate_email(email)
                new_email = email.strip()
                existing = self.users.by_email.get(new_email)
                if existing is not None and existing != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("email already in use")
                if new_email != user.email:
                    self.users.by_email.pop(user.email, None)
                    self.users.by_email[new_email] = user_id
                    user.email = new_email

            if role is not _UNSET:
                if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                    raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
                user.role = role

            return self._copy(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        """Delete a user.

        Refuses (``ConflictError``) when the user still owns a project or is the
        assignee of an open (non-completed) task.
        """
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            for project in self.projects.by_id.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError("user still owns a project")

            for task_id in self.tasks.by_assignee.get(user_id, set()):
                task = self.tasks.by_id.get(task_id)
                if task is not None and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError("user has open assigned tasks")

            self.tasks.by_assignee.pop(user_id, None)
            self.users.by_username.pop(user.username, None)
            self.users.by_email.pop(user.email, None)
            self.users.by_id.pop(user_id, None)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Project management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def create_project(
        self,
        name: str,
        start_date: datetime,
        end_date: datetime,
        owner_id: int,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Project:
        """Create a project owned by ``owner_id``."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(owner_id)
            if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
                raise ValidationError("project name must be a non-empty string")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot precede start_date")

            project = Project(
                id=self._next_id(),
                name=name.strip(),
                description=description or "",
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                owner_id=owner_id,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.projects.by_id[project.id] = project
            self.tasks.by_project.setdefault(project.id, [])
            self.messages.by_project.setdefault(project.id, [])
            return self._copy(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        """Return a copy of the project with ``project_id``."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._copy(self._require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        """Return every project, sorted by id ascending."""
        with self._lock:
            return [
                self._copy(self.projects.by_id[pid])
                for pid in sorted(self.projects.by_id)
            ]

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        name: Any = _UNSET,
        description: Any = _UNSET,
        start_date: Any = _UNSET,
        end_date: Any = _UNSET,
    ) -> Project:
        """Update mutable fields of a project, returning the updated copy."""
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)

            new_start = project.start_date if start_date is _UNSET else start_date
            new_end = project.end_date if end_date is _UNSET else end_date
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot precede start_date")

            if name is not _UNSET:
                if not isinstance(name, str) or not name.strip():
                    raise ValidationError("project name must be a non-empty string")
                project.name = name.strip()
            if description is not _UNSET:
                project.description = description or ""
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            return self._copy(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        """Delete a project and cascade to its tasks and messages."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)

            for task_id in list(self.tasks.by_project.get(project_id, [])):
                self._purge_task(task_id)
            self.tasks.by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            for message_id in list(self.messages.by_project.get(project_id, [])):
                self.messages.by_id.pop(message_id, None)
            self.messages.by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            self.projects.by_id.pop(project_id, None)

    def _purge_task(self, task_id: int) -> None:
        """Remove a task and its task-scoped messages from every index."""
        task = self.tasks.by_id.pop(task_id, None)
        if task is None:
            return
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            assignees = self.tasks.by_assignee.get(task.assignee_id)
            if assignees is not None:
                assignees.discard(task_id)
        for message_id in list(self.messages.by_task.get(task_id, [])):
            self.messages.by_id.pop(message_id, None)
        self.messages.by_task.pop(task_id, None)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Task management
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def create_task(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        title: str,
        assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
        deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
    ) -> Task:
        """Create a task inside ``project_id``."""
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if not isinstance(title, str) or not title.strip():
                raise ValidationError("task title must be a non-empty string")
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline cannot precede project start")

            task = Task(
                id=self._next_id(),
                project_id=project_id,
                title=title.strip(),
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.tasks.by_id[task.id] = task
            self.tasks.by_project.setdefault(project_id, []).append(task.id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self.tasks.by_assignee.setdefault(assignee_id, set()).add(task.id)
            return self._copy(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        """Return a copy of the task with ``task_id``."""
        with self._lock:
            return self._copy(self._require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        """Return tasks for a project, sorted by id ascending."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self.tasks.by_project.get(project_id, []))
            return [self._copy(self.tasks.by_id[tid]) for tid in ids]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        """Return tasks assigned to a user, sorted by id ascending."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ids = sorted(self.tasks.by_assignee.get(user_id, set()))
            return [self._copy(self.tasks.by_id[tid]) for tid in ids]

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        """Assign (or move) a task to ``assignee_id``."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            self._move_assignment(task, assignee_id)
            return self._copy(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        """Re-assign a task to a different user (alias of :meth:`assign_task`)."""
        return self.assign_task(task_id, assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        """Remove any assignee from a task."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._move_assignment(task, None)
            return self._copy(task)

    def _move_assignment(self, task: Task, assignee_id: Optional[int]) -> None:
        """Update the assignee index when a task's assignee changes."""
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            current = self.tasks.by_assignee.get(task.assignee_id)
            if current is not None:
                current.discard(task.id)
        task.assignee_id = assignee_id
        if assignee_id is not None:
            self.tasks.by_assignee.setdefault(assignee_id, set()).add(task.id)

    def set_deadline(
        self, task_id: int, deadline: Optional[datetime]
    ) -> Task:
        """Set or clear a task's deadline."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self.projects.by_id.get(task.project_id)
                if project is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline cannot precede project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return self._copy(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        """Transition a task to ``status`` and maintain its timestamps."""
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")

            now = self._now()
            if status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED:
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now

            task.status = status
            return self._copy(task)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Messaging
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        body: str,
        project_id: Optional[int] = None,
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
        attachments: Optional[List[Tuple[str, bytes]]] = None,
        parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        """Post a message scoped to exactly one project or task."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)

            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "exactly one of project_id or task_id is required"
                )
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            attachments = attachments or []
            if not body.strip() and not attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message body or an attachment is required")
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            now = self._now()
            message = Message(
                id=self._next_id(),
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                created_at=now,
                edited_at=None,
                attachments=[],
            )
            for filename, data in attachments:
                message.attachments.append(
                    self._build_attachment(author_id, filename, data, now)
                )

            self.messages.by_id[message.id] = message
            if project_id is not None:
                self.messages.by_project.setdefault(project_id, []).append(message.id)
            else:
                self.messages.by_task.setdefault(task_id, []).append(message.id)
            return self._copy(message)

    def _build_attachment(
        self, uploader_id: int, filename: str, data: bytes, when: datetime
    ) -> Attachment:
        if not isinstance(filename, str) or not filename.strip():
            raise ValidationError("attachment filename must be non-empty")
        payload = data if data is not None else b""
        return Attachment(
            id=self._next_id(),
            filename=filename,
            size=len(payload),
            uploaded_by=uploader_id,
            uploaded_at=when,
            content=bytes(payload),
        )

    def edit_message(
        self, message_id: int, editor_id: int, new_body: str
    ) -> Message:
        """Edit a message body (author only)."""
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit a message")
            if not isinstance(new_body, str) or not new_body.strip():
                raise ValidationError("message body must be a non-empty string")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return self._copy(message)

    def add_attachment(
        self, message_id: int, uploader_id: int, filename: str, data: bytes
    ) -> Message:
        """Attach a file to an existing message (author only)."""
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "only the author may attach to a message"
                )
            attachment = self._build_attachment(
                uploader_id, filename, data, self._now()
            )
            message.attachments.append(attachment)
            return self._copy(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        """Return messages scoped to a project, sorted by id ascending."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            ids = sorted(self.messages.by_project.get(project_id, []))
            return [self._copy(self.messages.by_id[mid]) for mid in ids]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        """Return messages scoped to a task, sorted by id ascending."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            ids = sorted(self.messages.by_task.get(task_id, []))
            return [self._copy(self.messages.by_id[mid]) for mid in ids]

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, requester_id: int) -> None:
        """Delete a message (author only)."""
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete a message")
            if message.project_id is not None:
                refs = self.messages.by_project.get(message.project_id)
                if refs is not None and message_id in refs:
                    refs.remove(message_id)
            if message.task_id is not None:
                refs = self.messages.by_task.get(message.task_id)
                if refs is not None and message_id in refs:
                    refs.remove(message_id)
            self.messages.by_id.pop(message_id, None)

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Performance dashboard
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def _assignee_tasks(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        ids = self.tasks.by_assignee.get(user_id, set())
        return [self.tasks.by_id[tid] for tid in ids if tid in self.tasks.by_id]

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_seconds(task: Task) -> Optional[float]:
        if task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
            return None
        if task.started_at is None or task.completed_at is None:
            return None
        return (task.completed_at - task.started_at).total_seconds()

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        """Return completed / total assigned tasks for a user (0.0 if none)."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assignee_tasks(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            return completed / len(tasks)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        """Return the mean completion time in seconds, or ``None`` if no data."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            durations = [
                d
                for d in (self._completion_seconds(t) for t in self._assignee_tasks(user_id))
                if d is not None
            ]
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def submit_rating(
        self,
        rater_id: int,
        ratee_id: int,
        score: int,
        feedback: str = "",
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Rating:
        """Record a peer rating (1-5) for ``ratee_id``."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("a user cannot rate themselves")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)
            if isinstance(score, bool) or not isinstance(score, int):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer between 1 and 5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")

            rating = Rating(
                id=self._next_id(),
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.performance.ratings_by_ratee.setdefault(ratee_id, []).append(rating)
            return self._copy(rating)

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Return ``{count, average, ratings}`` for a user's received ratings."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ratings = self.performance.ratings_by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count if count else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [self._copy(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Return a consolidated performance dashboard for a user."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._assignee_tasks(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            durations = [
                d for d in (self._completion_seconds(t) for t in tasks)
                if d is not None
            ]
            avg_time = (
                sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
            )
            ratings = self.performance.ratings_by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Reporting
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Build a progress report for a single project."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            now = self._now()
            task_ids = self.tasks.by_project.get(project_id, [])
            tasks = [self.tasks.by_id[tid] for tid in task_ids]

            status_counts = {s.value: 0 for s in TaskStatus}
            overdue = 0
            per_assignee: Dict[int, Dict[str, Any]] = {}
            completed_total = 0

            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed_total += 1
                if (
                    task.deadline is not None
                    and task.deadline < now
                    and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                ):
                    overdue += 1
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                        task.assignee_id, {"total": 0, "completed": 0}
                    )
                    bucket["total"] += 1
                    if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                        bucket["completed"] += 1

            for bucket in per_assignee.values():
                bucket["completion_rate"] = (
                    bucket["completed"] / bucket["total"]
                    if bucket["total"]
                    else 0.0
                )

            total = len(tasks)
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "generated_at": now,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": (completed_total / total) if total else 0.0,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(
        self, project_id: Optional[int] = None
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Build a team-wide performance report, optionally scoped to a project."""
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                scoped = [
                    self.tasks.by_id[tid]
                    for tid in self.tasks.by_project.get(project_id, [])
                ]
            else:
                scoped = list(self.tasks.by_id.values())

            by_user: Dict[int, List[Task]] = {}
            for task in scoped:
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    by_user.setdefault(task.assignee_id, []).append(task)

            rows: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
            for user_id in sorted(by_user):
                tasks = by_user[user_id]
                total = len(tasks)
                completed = sum(
                    1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                )
                durations = [
                    d for d in (self._completion_seconds(t) for t in tasks)
                    if d is not None
                ]
                avg_time = (
                    sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
                )
                user = self.users.by_id.get(user_id)
                ratings = self.performance.ratings_by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
                rating_average = (
                    sum(r.score for r in ratings) / len(ratings)
                    if ratings
                    else 0.0
                )
                rows.append(
                    {
                        "user_id": user_id,
                        "username": user.username if user else "",
                        "total_tasks": total,
                        "completed_tasks": completed,
                        "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                        "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                        "rating_average": rating_average,
                    }
                )

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "generated_at": self._now(),
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Build a detailed performance report for a single user."""
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = sorted(self._assignee_tasks(user_id), key=lambda t: t.id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            durations = [
                d for d in (self._completion_seconds(t) for t in tasks)
                if d is not None
            ]
            avg_time = sum(durations) / len(durations) if durations else None
            ratings = self.performance.ratings_by_ratee.get(user_id, [])
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / len(ratings) if ratings else 0.0
            )

            task_rows = [
                {
                    "task_id": t.id,
                    "title": t.title,
                    "status": t.status.value,
                    "project_id": t.project_id,
                    "completed_at": t.completed_at,
                }
                for t in tasks
            ]

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "generated_at": self._now(),
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "average_completion_time": avg_time,
                "rating_count": len(ratings),
                "rating_average": rating_average,
                "tasks": task_rows,
            }

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    # Export
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def export_report(self, report: Dict[str, Any], fmt: ReportFormat):
        """Render a report dict to CSV (``str``) or PDF (``bytes``)."""
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(report, dict):
                raise ValidationError("report must be a dict")
            if not isinstance(fmt, ReportFormat):
                raise ValidationError("fmt must be a ReportFormat")

            title, header, rows = self._report_table(report)
            if fmt == ReportFormat.CSV:
                return self._render_csv(header, rows)
            return self._render_pdf(title, header, rows)

    @staticmethod
    def _report_table(
        report: Dict[str, Any]
    ) -> Tuple[str, List[str], List[List[Any]]]:
        """Flatten a report dict into ``(title, header, rows)`` for export."""
        rtype = report.get("report_type")

        if rtype == ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value:
            header = [
                "user_id",
                "username",
                "total_tasks",
                "completed_tasks",
                "completion_rate",
                "average_completion_time",
                "rating_average",
            ]
            rows = [
                [row.get(col, "") for col in header]
                for row in report.get("rows", [])
            ]
            return "Team Performance Report", header, rows

        if rtype == ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value:
            header = ["metric", "value"]
            rows: List[List[Any]] = [
                ["project_id", report.get("project_id")],
                ["total_tasks", report.get("total_tasks")],
                ["completion_rate", report.get("completion_rate")],
                ["overdue_tasks", report.get("overdue_tasks")],
            ]
            for status, count in report.get("status_counts", {}).items():
                rows.append(["status:" + status, count])
            for assignee_id, stats in report.get("per_assignee", {}).items():
                rows.append(
                    [
                        "assignee:" + str(assignee_id),
                        "{0}/{1}".format(
                            stats.get("completed"), stats.get("total")
                        ),
                    ]
                )
            return "Project Progress Report", header, rows

        if rtype == ReportType.INDIVIDUAL.value:
            header = ["task_id", "title", "status", "completed_at"]
            rows = [
                [
                    t.get("task_id"),
                    t.get("title"),
                    t.get("status"),
                    t.get("completed_at"),
                ]
                for t in report.get("tasks", [])
            ]
            return "Individual Performance Report", header, rows

        # Generic fallback: dump the dict as key/value rows.
        header = ["key", "value"]
        rows = [[key, report[key]] for key in report]
        return "Report", header, rows

    @staticmethod
    def _render_csv(header: List[str], rows: List[List[Any]]) -> str:
        buffer = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(buffer)
        writer.writerow(header)
        for row in rows:
            writer.writerow(row)
        return buffer.getvalue()

    @staticmethod
    def _render_pdf(title: str, header: List[str], rows: List[List[Any]]) -> bytes:
        """Produce a minimal, valid single-page PDF document."""
        lines = [title, ", ".join(str(col) for col in header)]
        for row in rows:
            lines.append(", ".join("" if v is None else str(v) for v in row))

        content_ops = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf"]
        y = 760
        for line in lines:
            safe = (
                line.replace("\\", "\\\\")
                .replace("(", "\\(")
                .replace(")", "\\)")
            )
            content_ops.append("1 0 0 1 50 {0} Tm ({1}) Tj".format(y, safe))
            y -= 16
            if y < 40:
                break
        content_ops.append("ET")
        content = "\n".join(content_ops).encode("latin-1", "replace")

        objects = [
            b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
            b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
            (
                b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
                b"/Contents 4 0 R /Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> >>"
            ),
            b"<< /Length "
            + str(len(content)).encode("ascii")
            + b" >>\nstream\n"
            + content
            + b"\nendstream",
            b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        ]

        pdf = bytearray(b"%PDF-1.4\n")
        offsets: List[int] = []
        for index, body in enumerate(objects, start=1):
            offsets.append(len(pdf))
            pdf += "{0} 0 obj\n".format(index).encode("ascii")
            pdf += body
            pdf += b"\nendobj\n"

        xref_pos = len(pdf)
        size = len(objects) + 1
        pdf += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(size).encode("ascii")
        pdf += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
        for offset in offsets:
            pdf += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset).encode("ascii")
        pdf += (
            "trailer\n<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\nstartxref\n{1}\n%%EOF".format(
                size, xref_pos
            ).encode("ascii")
        )
        return bytes(pdf)


# Friendly aliases that expose the manager under the Science_Collaboratory name
# requested by the product brief.  They reference the very same class.
ScienceCollaboratory = TeamCollaborationManager
Science_Collaboratory = TeamCollaborationManager


def _demo() -> None:  # pragma: no cover - illustrative CLI dashboard only
    """Tiny textual dashboard demonstrating the collaboration workflow."""
    mgr = TeamCollaborationManager()
    lead = mgr.register_user("ada", "ada@lab.org", UserRole.ADMIN)
    analyst = mgr.register_user("rosalind", "rosa@lab.org")
    start = datetime.now()
    project = mgr.create_project(
        "Protein Folding Study",
        start,
        start + timedelta(days=365),
        lead.id,
        "Collaborative analysis of folding stability.",
    )
    task = mgr.create_task(project.id, "Clean experimental dataset", analyst.id)
    mgr.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
    mgr.post_message(lead.id, "Kick-off: let's get the data cleaned.", project_id=project.id)

    print("=" * 60)
    print("  SCIENCE COLLABORATORY - DASHBOARD")
    print("=" * 60)
    for user in mgr.list_users():
        dash = mgr.get_user_dashboard(user.id)
        bar_len = 20
        filled = int(round(dash["completion_rate"] * bar_len))
        bar = "#" * filled + "-" * (bar_len - filled)
        print(
            "  {0:<12} role={1:<6} tasks={2}  [{3}] {4:.0%}".format(
                user.username,
                user.role.value,
                dash["total_tasks"],
                bar,
                dash["completion_rate"],
            )
        )
    report = mgr.project_progress_report(project.id)
    print("-" * 60)
    print(
        "  Project '{0}': {1} task(s), {2:.0%} complete, {3} overdue".format(
            project.name,
            report["total_tasks"],
            report["completion_rate"],
            report["overdue_tasks"],
        )
    )
    print("=" * 60)


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    _demo()
